item.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.

item.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.

item.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

item.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

item.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

item.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

item.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
item.dc:creator: Natasha Lomas, Romain Dillet, Kyle Wiggers, Lucas Ropek
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

item.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

item.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

item.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

item.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

item.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

item.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

item.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.

item.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.

item.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the infrastructure and energy that power them.

item.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

item.title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.
